tasks.prompt: Read the repository and explain the active Renderer -> preload -> main -> Kun runtime data path. Cite the most relevant file paths. Do not modify files.
Find the Kun serve-mode composition root and summarize how stores, model clients, tools, and the agent loop are assembled. Cite exact file paths. Do not modify files.
Trace a chat message from the renderer composer through the preload/main bridge to the Kun turn endpoint. Return a concise ordered call path with files. Do not modify files.
Explain how Kun SSE event replay avoids duplicates and cursor rewind after reconnect or restart. Cite the implementation and tests. Do not modify files.
Inspect MCP startup, tool discovery, execution, and reconnect behavior. Identify the main reliability boundaries and cite the implementation files. Do not modify files.
Explain what makes Kun's immutable prompt prefix stable and list dynamic data that must remain outside it. Cite code and documentation. Do not modify files.
Trace how baseUrl and endpointFormat affect provider URL construction and request bodies across chat and auxiliary model calls. Cite all important consumers. Do not modify files.
Trace an image or local file attachment from renderer selection to model input or fallback. Identify the cross-layer contract fields and failure points. Do not modify files.
Trace a tool approval request from agent loop creation through SSE/UI resolution back to tool execution. Cite routes, gates, and renderer handlers. Do not modify files.
Explain how active goals survive runtime restart, how orphaned turns are reconciled, and where auto-resume is triggered. Cite tests if present. Do not modify files.
Explain how subagent tool policies inherit or restrict built-in tools, MCP servers, and skills without escalating the parent permissions. Cite enforcement points. Do not modify files.
Trace a Kun settings change from renderer state through validation/persistence to managed runtime restart. Highlight rollback behavior. Do not modify files.
Identify how the verify_changes tool selects and runs validation after edits. Explain its safety limits and output contract. Do not modify files.
Summarize the development, typecheck, test, build, and packaging pipeline for Kun. Cite package scripts and packaging configuration. Do not modify files.
Map the main trust boundaries for renderer IPC, filesystem tools, command execution, MCP, and secrets. Cite concrete enforcement files. Do not modify files.
Inspect runtime event persistence, SSE replay, tool execution, and context assembly. Identify three evidence-based performance or memory hotspots with file references. Do not modify files.
Explain how thread/session data is persisted and indexed across file and hybrid SQLite stores, including usage carryover. Cite implementation files. Do not modify files.
Explain how model capabilities control image input, tool calling, reasoning effort, endpoint format, and context limits. Cite schemas and request construction. Do not modify files.
Inspect renderer lazy loading and identify which Workbench surfaces are split into separate chunks and which heavy chat dependencies still load eagerly. Do not modify files.
Map how the desktop app detects an unhealthy Kun child, budgets restarts, distinguishes settings restarts from crashes, and reports status to the renderer. Do not modify files.
